title: Production and process engineers
description: Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. Design chemical plant equipment and devise processes for manufacturing chemicals and products, such as gasoline, synthetic rubber, plastics, detergents, cement, paper, and pulp, by applying principles and technology of chemistry, physics, and engineering. Research, design, develop, or test computer or computer-related equipment for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use. May supervise the manufacturing and installation of computer or computer-related equipment and components. (Based on 22 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Perform tests and monitor performance of processes throughout stages of production to determine degree of control over variables such as temperature, density, specific gravity, and pressure. Monitor and analyze data from processes and experiments. Evaluate chemical equipment and processes to identify ways to optimize performance or to ensure compliance with safety and environmental regulations. Troubleshoot problems with chemical manufacturing processes. Investigate or resolve operational problems, such as material use variances or bottlenecks. Analyze statistical data and product specifications to determine standards and establish quality and reliability objectives of finished product. Recommend resolution of identified deviations from established product or process standards. Conduct validation or qualification tests of new or existing processes, equipment, or software in accordance with internal protocols or external standards. Communicate with management and user personnel to develop production and design standards. Prepare reports summarizing information or trends related to manufacturing performance.

Develop safety procedures to be employed by workers operating equipment or working in close proximity to ongoing chemical reactions. Inspect work sites to identify physical hazards. Review health, safety, accident, or worker compensation records to evaluate safety program effectiveness or to identify jobs with high incidence of injury. Recommend workplace changes to improve health and safety, using knowledge of potentially harmful factors, such as heavy loads or repetitive motions.

Develop computer models of chemical processes. Design measurement and control systems for chemical plants based on data collected in laboratory experiments and in pilot plant operations. Conduct research to develop new and improved chemical manufacturing processes. Build, test, and modify product prototypes, using working models or theoretical models constructed with computer simulation. Develop or implement research methodologies or statistical analysis plans to test and evaluate developmental prototypes used in new products or processes, such as cockpit designs, user workstations, or computerized human models. Create, populate, or maintain databases for tracking validation activities, test results, or validated systems.

Prepare estimate of production costs and production progress reports for management. Estimate time or resource requirements for ergonomic or human factors research or development projects. Estimate costs, production times, or staffing requirements for new designs. Prepare budget or cost estimates for equipment, construction, or installation projects or control expenditures. Prepare, maintain, or review validation and compliance documentation, such as engineering change notices, schematics, or protocols.

Design and plan layout of equipment. Determine most effective arrangement of operations such as mixing, crushing, heat transfer, distillation, and drying. Plan and establish sequence of operations to fabricate and assemble parts or products and to promote efficient utilization. Draft and design layout of equipment, materials, and workspace to illustrate maximum efficiency using drafting tools and computer. Validate or characterize sustainable or environmentally friendly products, using electronic testing platforms. Design layout of equipment or workspaces to achieve maximum efficiency.

Conduct interviews or surveys of users or customers to collect information on topics, such as requirements, needs, fatigue, ergonomics, or interfaces. Analyze user needs and recommend appropriate hardware. Analyze the financial impacts of sustainable manufacturing processes or sustainable product manufacturing. Advocate for end users in collaboration with other professionals, including engineers, designers, managers, or customers. Evaluate factors such as reporting formats required, cost constraints, and need for security restrictions to determine hardware configuration. Determine root causes of failures or recommend changes in designs, tolerances, or processing methods, using statistical procedures. Analyze complex systems to determine potential for further development, production, interoperability, compatibility, or usefulness in a particular area, such as aviation.

Train production personnel in new or existing methods. Provide technical support to clients through activities, such as rearranging workplace fixtures to reduce physical hazards or discomfort or modifying task sequences to reduce cycle time. Supervise technicians, technologists, analysts, administrative staff, or other engineers. Direct activities of workers who operate or are engaged in constructing and improving absorption, evaporation, or electromagnetic equipment. Provide technical expertise or support related to manufacturing. Direct workers engaged in product measurement, inspection, and testing activities to ensure quality control and reliability. Develop or implement human performance research, investigation, or analysis protocols.

Review product designs for manufacturability or completeness. Collaborate with engineers, designers, managers, or customers to discuss existing or potential projects or products. Evaluate current or proposed manufacturing processes or practices for environmental sustainability, considering factors such as greenhouse gas emissions, air pollution, water pollution, energy use, or waste creation. Design cognitive aids, such as procedural storyboards or decision support systems. Communicate manufacturing capabilities, production schedules, or other information to facilitate production processes. Implement methods and procedures for disposition of discrepant material and defective or damaged parts, and assess cost and responsibility.

Incorporate new manufacturing methods or processes to improve existing operations. Identify opportunities or implement changes to improve manufacturing processes or products or to reduce costs, using knowledge of fabrication processes, tooling and production equipment, assembly methods, quality control standards, or product design, materials and parts. Analyze the impacts of product design changes on expenditures for management review, action, and control. Provide human factors technical expertise on topics, such as advanced user-interface technology development or the role of human users in automated or autonomous sub-systems in advanced vehicle systems. Train users in task techniques or ergonomic principles. Provide training and support to system designers and users. Update knowledge and skills to keep up with rapid advancements in computer technology.

Conduct research to evaluate potential solutions related to changes in equipment design, procedures, manpower, personnel, or training. Prepare detailed reports or design statements based on results of validation and qualification tests or reviews of procedures and protocols. Prepare documentation containing information such as confidential descriptions or specifications of proprietary hardware or software, product development or introduction schedules, product costs, or information about product performance weaknesses. Write, review, or comment on documents, such as proposals, test plans, or procedures.
Remove tasks clearly irrelevant to this occupation.

Provide technical support to designers, marketing and sales departments, suppliers, engineers and other team members throughout the product development and implementation process. Ensure compliance with safety and environmental regulations. Analyze electronics system requirements, capacity, cost, or customer needs to determine project feasibility. Develop validation master plans, process flow diagrams, test cases, or standard operating procedures.
Test radio frequency identification device (RFID) software and hardware to ensure proper functioning, including tags or labels, and perform systems analysis or programming of RFID technology.

Develop and document process flows, work instructions, or standard operating procedures for radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems, and define and compare possible RFID solutions for specific projects.

Provide technical support for radio frequency identification device (RFID) technology, including training users, educating clinicians and other personnel on proper equipment use, and assisting in the integration of RFID solutions into other applications.
Create simulations or models of radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems, and develop methodologies for transferring procedures from laboratories to commercial-scale manufacturing.
Conduct testing and maintain systems that integrate RFID technologies, including reading current literature and collaborating with colleagues to stay informed about industry developments.
Oversee and provide expertise on manufacturing, assembly, or fabrication processes, and manage teams of engineers by creating schedules and budgets.

Design and develop medical diagnostic or clinical instrumentation, equipment, or procedures using principles of engineering and biobehavioral sciences, and conduct research related to the evaluation of safety levels for products.
Evaluate potential health hazards associated with product misuse and recommend procedures for detection, prevention, and elimination of product hazards.
Prepare technical reports of findings or recommendations based on analyses of test results, and participate in the preparation of product usage and precautionary label instructions.
Monitor compliance with safety regulations and standards and conduct or coordinate training in safety laws and hazard monitoring for workers.
Analyze system performance or operational requirements, and evaluate adequacy of actions taken to correct health inspection violations or safety issues.
Design optical or imaging systems, including developing photonics prototypes and determining applications of photonics to meet product objectives or features.
Conduct research to identify improvements for industrial processes, and analyze, fabricate, or test fiber-optic links and related technologies.
Prepare energy-related project reports and conduct energy audits to evaluate energy use, identify conservation measures, and recommend energy savings strategies for efficient operation.

Collaborate with manufacturing or quality assurance staff to prepare product specifications, safety sheets, and standard operating procedures, ensuring compatibility of design and production processes.
Train personnel or clients on topics such as energy management and safety equipment use, and read current literature to stay abreast of technological advances.
Prepare and write reports detailing specific issues regarding product safety and compliance, including conducting research on emerging technologies and industry standards.
Investigate industrial accidents or occupational diseases to determine causes and recommend preventive measures.
Conduct environmental studies related to pollution remediation and develop methodologies to minimize the environmental impact of products.
Direct the implementation of energy management projects, and design or develop automated control systems for environmental applications.
Maintain databases of research and experimental results, documenting design processes, objectives, and outcomes for future reference.

Upgrade the design of existing devices by adding mechatronic elements, create embedded software design programs, design advanced electronic control systems for mechanical systems, and design engineering systems for the automation of industrial tasks.

Develop or file intellectual property and patent documents related to microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) devices, and conduct or oversee prototype development or microfabrication activities to ensure compliance with specifications and promote effective production processes.

Research, select, or apply sensors, communication technologies, or control devices for motion control, position sensing, pressure sensing, or electronic communication; communicate operating characteristics or performance experience to other engineers or designers for training or new product development purposes.

Develop formal documentation for microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) devices, including quality assurance guidance, quality control protocols, process control checklists, data collection, or reporting, and create or maintain formal engineering documents, such as schematics, bills of materials, components or materials specifications, or packaging requirements.

Conduct analyses addressing issues such as failure, reliability, or yield improvement; analyze existing development or manufacturing procedures and suggest improvements; evaluate technical specifications and economic factors relating to process or product design objectives.

Investigate characteristics such as cost, performance, or process capability of potential microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) device designs, using simulation or modeling software; and review specifications and recommend engineering or manufacturing changes to achieve design objectives.

Propose product designs involving microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) technology, optimize design for target dimensions, physical tolerances, or processing constraints; and develop or validate product-specific test protocols, acceptance thresholds, or inspection tools for quality control testing or performance measurement.
Supervise technologists, technicians, or other engineers.
Create models to optimize the layout of wind farm access roads, crane pads, and transmission lines; and create or maintain wind farm layouts, schematics, or other visual documentation for wind farms.

Analyze operation of wind farms or wind farm components to determine reliability, performance, and compliance with specifications; and monitor wind farm construction to ensure compliance with regulatory standards or environmental requirements.

Design or develop nuclear equipment, such as reactor cores, radiation shielding, or associated instrumentation or control mechanisms; and oversee the work of contractors in accordance with project requirements.

Conduct engineering site audits to collect structural, electrical, and related site information for use in the design of residential or commercial solar power systems, and provide technical direction or support to installation teams during installation, start-up, testing, system commissioning, or performance monitoring.

Develop design specifications and functional requirements for solar energy systems or components, and design or coordinate design of photovoltaic (PV) systems including system components for residential and commercial buildings.
Design robotic systems, such as automatic vehicle control or advanced sensing systems; and automate assays on laboratory robotics.

Create high-resolution images or measure force-distance curves using techniques such as atomic force microscopy; and provide scientific or technical guidance or expertise to scientists, engineers, technologists, technicians, or others on micro and nanoscale systems.

Design and direct the testing or control of processing procedures, and supervise production and testing processes in industrial settings such as metal refining facilities, smelting or foundry operations, or nonmetallic materials production operations.
Supervise the work of technologists, technicians, and other engineers and scientists.
Monitor material performance, and evaluate its deterioration.
Analyze product failure data and laboratory test results to determine causes of problems and develop solutions.
